conventions.content: All variables, functions, and modules use snake_case. Classes use PascalCase. Constants use UPPER_CASE.

conventions.summary: Always use explicit error handling with context
conventions.content: Team convention is to catch specific exceptions and add contextual information before re-raising. Avoid bare except clauses. Log errors with structured data including request IDs.

conventions.summary: Configuration stored as JSON not YAML
conventions.content: Project uses JSON for all config files to avoid YAML parsing ambiguities. Config validation happens at startup with clear error messages.

conventions.summary: Use Google-style docstrings for all functions
conventions.content: Team standard is Google-style docstrings with Args, Returns, Raises sections. Include type hints in function signatures, not docstrings. Examples required for public API functions.
Import blocks separated by blank lines in order: standard library, third-party packages, local modules. Within each block, alphabetically sorted. Use isort for automatic formatting.
Project uses 100 character line limit instead of PEP 8's 79. Configured in .editorconfig and enforced by black formatter. Comments and docstrings follow same limit.

conventions.summary: Use /editor or Ctrl+J for multi-line prompts
conventions.content: In Kiro CLI chat, use /editor command or Ctrl+J to enter multi-line statements. /editor opens default editor (vi). /reply opens editor with previous assistant message quoted.
Kiro CLI automatically saves conversations based on working directory. Use --resume to continue previous conversation in current directory, or --resume-picker for interactive selection.

architecture.summary: Code-free RAG memory system using ripgrep
architecture.content: Project uses a grep-based memory system stored in .kiro/memory/insights.json. Agents use rg to search for relevant insights instead of reading the entire file, minimizing context usage.

architecture.summary: System uses event-driven architecture with message bus
architecture.content: Core components communicate via async message bus. Events are immutable and include correlation IDs for tracing. Enables loose coupling and independent scaling of services.

architecture.summary: Extensible plugin system with dynamic loading
architecture.content: Plugins discovered via entry points and loaded at runtime. Each plugin has isolated namespace and can register handlers, commands, and hooks without modifying core code.

architecture.summary: Two-tier caching with memory and disk layers
architecture.content: Caching architecture uses in-memory LRU cache as L1 and disk-based cache as L2. Hot data stays in memory, warm data on disk. Cache keys include version hash to auto-invalidate on code changes.

architecture.summary: MCP extends Kiro with external tools and context
architecture.content: Model Context Protocol allows Kiro to connect to specialized servers providing additional tools. MCP servers configured in .kiro/settings/mcp.json (workspace) or ~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json (user). Each server defined with command, args, and env vars.

architecture.summary: Custom agents define tool access and permissions
architecture.content: Custom agents in Kiro CLI specify which tools are available, pre-approved permissions, and context to include. Reduces permission prompts and tailors behavior for specific workflows. Agents can use built-in tools and MCP servers.

architecture.summary: Hooks execute custom commands at agent lifecycle points
architecture.content: Kiro hooks run at specific events: AgentSpawn, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop. Hooks receive JSON via STDIN with event context. Exit code 0 = success, 2 = block tool (PreToolUse only). Enables validation, logging, and custom behaviors.

patterns.summary: Use targeted retrieval instead of full file reads
patterns.content: When accessing memory, extract 2-4 key search terms and use rg to find only relevant sections. This preserves context window for actual work.

patterns.summary: Use context managers for resource cleanup
patterns.content: All file handles, network connections, and locks use context managers. Custom resources implement __enter__ and __exit__. Ensures cleanup even during exceptions.

patterns.summary: Expensive resources initialized lazily on first use
patterns.content: Database connections, API clients, and large data structures use lazy initialization pattern. Reduces startup time and memory footprint when features aren't used.

patterns.summary: Builder pattern for complex configuration objects
patterns.content: Complex configs use builder pattern with fluent interface. Enables step-by-step construction with validation at each step. Final build() method validates complete configuration.

patterns.summary: Hook matchers use tool names or aliases
patterns.content: Kiro hooks use matcher field to specify tools: canonical names (fs_write, execute_bash) or aliases (write, shell). MCP tools use @server/tool format. Special matchers: * (all tools), @builtin (built-in only), @server (all from MCP server).

decisions.summary: Chose grep-based search over ML embeddings
decisions.content: Decision to use ripgrep for memory retrieval instead of vector embeddings. Rationale: simpler, faster, no dependencies, sufficient for keyword-based retrieval, and preserves context window.

decisions.summary: Using SQLite for local storage instead of PostgreSQL
decisions.content: Chose SQLite for simplicity and zero-config deployment. Project doesn't need concurrent writes or network access. Can migrate to Postgres later if scaling requires it.

decisions.summary: Raw SQL queries instead of ORM
decisions.content: Team decided against using an ORM to maintain query visibility and performance control. SQL queries are in dedicated query modules with clear documentation.
All services in single monorepo for easier refactoring and atomic changes across services. Shared code in common packages. CI/CD detects changed services and deploys only those.

decisions.summary: MCP chosen for extensibility over hardcoded tools
decisions.content: Kiro uses Model Context Protocol to allow external tool integration instead of hardcoding all tools. Enables users to add custom tools without modifying Kiro core. Balances built-in tools for common tasks with MCP for specialized needs.

gotchas.content: Reading the full memory file wastes context space. Always use rg with specific search terms to retrieve only relevant insights. This is critical for maintaining efficient context usage.

gotchas.summary: Memory system tested and verified working
gotchas.content: System successfully tested on 2026-02-13. Ripgrep retrieval works correctly, can add new insights, and metadata updates properly. All core functionality confirmed operational.

gotchas.summary: Async context variables don't propagate to thread pools
gotchas.content: When using asyncio with thread pool executors, context variables are not automatically copied. Must explicitly pass context or use contextvars.copy_context() before thread execution.

gotchas.summary: datetime objects need custom JSON serialization
gotchas.content: Standard json.dumps fails on datetime objects. Project uses custom JSONEncoder that converts datetime to ISO format strings. Import from utils.json_helpers module.
Python's mutable default arguments are shared across calls. Always use None as default and initialize inside function. Linter configured to catch this pattern.

gotchas.summary: Redis connections must use connection pool
gotchas.content: Creating new Redis connection per request exhausts file descriptors. Always use connection pool initialized at startup. Pool size configured based on worker count.
Naive datetime objects cause subtle bugs across timezones. All datetime objects must include timezone info. Use UTC internally, convert to local only for display.

gotchas.summary: Use parameterized queries, never string formatting
gotchas.content: Never use f-strings or % formatting for SQL queries. Always use parameterized queries with placeholders. Linter flags string formatting in SQL query files.

gotchas.summary: Avoid circular imports with late imports
gotchas.content: Circular imports cause import errors. Move imports inside functions if needed, or restructure to remove circular dependency. Type hints can use string literals to avoid imports.

Kiro's /chat save and /chat load commands work independently of directory-based persistence. Loading a conversation replaces current one regardless of where it was saved. Use -f flag to force overwrite when saving.

gotchas.summary: Hook exit codes have specific meanings
gotchas.content: Kiro hook exit codes: 0 = success (STDOUT captured), 2 = block tool execution (PreToolUse only, STDERR to LLM), other = warning (STDERR shown to user). PreToolUse is only hook that can block tool execution.
Kiro hooks cache successful results based on cache_ttl_seconds: 0 = no caching (default), >0 = cache for specified seconds. AgentSpawn hooks are never cached. Default timeout is 30 seconds.

In custom agents, includeMcpJson field determines if agent inherits MCP servers from workspace/user mcp.json files. When true, agent gets both agent-defined and workspace MCP servers. When false, only agent-defined servers available.
